name: 约瑟夫·海顿
brief: 海顿出生于奥地利东部的罗劳村，家境贫寒却天赋过人。八岁进入维也纳圣斯蒂芬大教堂唱诗班，变声后一度生活困窘，靠教琴和为作曲家波尔波拉伴奏为生。1761年他成为匈牙利埃斯特哈齐亲王的宫廷乐长，在这个「远离世界的实验室」中度过了近三十年，自由地进行音乐实验。晚年两次访问伦敦，获得巨大成功，被称为「海顿爸爸」，是当时全欧洲最受尊敬的作曲家。
briefEn: Haydn was born in the village of Rohrau in eastern Austria to a humble family, yet possessed extraordinary talent. At eight he joined the choir of St. Stephen's Cathedral in Vienna; after his voice broke, he struggled to make ends meet by teaching and accompanying the composer Porpora. In 1761 he became Kapellmeister to the Hungarian Prince Esterhazy, spending nearly thirty years in what he called his 'laboratory cut off from the world,' free to experiment with music. His two late visits to London brought him immense acclaim, and he became known affectionately as 'Papa Haydn' — the most respected composer in all of Europe.
contribution: 海顿被称为「交响曲之父」和「弦乐四重奏之父」。他创作了104部交响曲和68首弦乐四重奏，将这两种体裁从简单的娱乐音乐发展成为严肃的艺术形式。他完善了奏鸣曲式的结构原则——呈示、展开、再现——这成为古典音乐最重要的形式基础。他的主题发展技巧（Thematic Development）影响了此后每一位作曲家。
contributionEn: Haydn is hailed as the 'Father of the Symphony' and the 'Father of the String Quartet.' With 104 symphonies and 68 string quartets, he transformed both genres from light entertainment into serious art forms. He refined the structural principles of sonata form — exposition, development, recapitulation — establishing the most important formal foundation in classical music. His technique of thematic development influenced every composer who followed.
style: 海顿的音乐以智慧、幽默和出人意料的创造力著称。他喜欢在音乐中制造惊喜——假结尾、突然的力度变化（如「惊愕」交响曲中著名的大声和弦）、意想不到的转调。他的慢乐章深情而不煽情，小步舞曲充满乡村式的活力。他善于用简单的主题素材通过巧妙的发展手法构建宏大的音乐结构。
styleEn: Haydn's music is celebrated for its wit, humor, and endlessly inventive surprises. He delighted in musical mischief — false endings, sudden dynamic shifts (such as the famous loud chord in the 'Surprise' Symphony), and unexpected modulations. His slow movements are heartfelt without being sentimental, and his minuets brim with rustic vitality. He had a remarkable gift for building grand musical structures from the simplest thematic material through ingenious development.

famousScores.name: 《第94号交响曲「惊愕」》
famousScores.desc: 慢乐章中突如其来的大声和弦，音乐幽默的经典
famousScores.descEn: A classic of musical humor, with its famous sudden fortissimo chord in the slow movement
famousScores.detail:
《第94号交响曲》（G大调「惊愕」）是海顿1791年访问伦敦期间为第一次伦敦之行所作的12首「伦敦交响曲」之一，1792年3月在汉诺威广场音乐厅首演。这首交响曲的第二乐章以甜美温柔的主题开场，就在听众几乎要昏昏入睡之际，管弦乐队突然爆发出一个震耳欲聋的全奏强音和弦，将人从睡意中惊醒。

关于这个「惊愕」效果的由来，流传着两个版本：海顿本人说他只是想写一首难忘的主题，以防人们把它与他其他的慢乐章主题混淆；另一个版本则说他是专门为了捉弄那些在慢乐章中打瞌睡的伦敦贵族听众。无论真相如何，这一音效在首演时引发了轰动，成为海顿和伦敦观众之间最广为人知的「玩笑」，据说许多人专程返场只为听那一声惊天大响。

「惊愕」交响曲是海顿在古典交响曲范畴内幽默精神的集中体现。它的终曲以极为精练的主题材料进行了令人叹服的发展——短短几个音符在各声部间传递变形，展示了海顿主题发展技艺的高超境界。这首交响曲的成功使海顿成为伦敦的音乐英雄，牛津大学因此授予他荣誉音乐博士学位（尽管「惊愕」与此授衔无关，但两件事发生在同一次伦敦之行中）。
famousScores.detailEn:
Symphony No. 94 in G major 'Surprise' was composed during Haydn's first London visit in 1791, premiering at the Hanover Square Rooms in March 1792. The slow movement opens with a delicate, gentle theme — and just as the audience is lulled into drowsy contentment, the full orchestra explodes in a thunderous fortissimo chord that jolts everyone awake.

Two competing stories explain the 'surprise': Haydn himself said he simply wanted an unforgettable theme to distinguish it from his other slow movements; the alternative tradition claims he devised it specifically to mock the London aristocrats who habitually dozed through andante movements. Whatever the truth, the effect caused a sensation at the premiere, and audiences reportedly returned specifically to hear that single shattering chord — making it the most famous joke in the history of the symphony.

The 'Surprise' is the quintessential embodiment of Haydn's wit within the Classical symphonic framework. Its finale demonstrates his thematic development at its most economical and brilliant — a handful of notes passed between voices, transformed and recombined with seemingly effortless ingenuity. The symphony's enormous success in London contributed to Haydn being awarded an honorary doctorate from Oxford University during the same visit, cementing his status as the most celebrated living composer in Europe.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Haydn Symphony 94 Surprise Leonard Bernstein Vienna

famousScores.name: 《第104号交响曲「伦敦」》
famousScores.desc: 最后一部交响曲，古典交响曲的巅峰之作
famousScores.descEn: His final symphony and the crowning achievement of the Classical symphonic tradition
famousScores.detail:
《第104号交响曲》（D大调「伦敦」）是海顿1795年在伦敦创作的最后一部交响曲，也是他104部交响曲生涯的终点。作品以一个严肃庄重的慢速引子开始，在宏大与沉思之间建立张力，随后奏鸣曲式的快板主部活力四射。终乐章建立在一段简单的克罗地亚民歌主题之上（海顿本人的民俗记忆），却被发展为一首气势宏伟的交响终章。

这首交响曲的演出条件颇具时代感：1795年5月，海顿在伦敦「国王剧院」指挥了首演，演出是一场盛大的「受益人音乐会」（benefit concert），海顿在英国的最后一次亮相。那晚的音乐会大获成功，据报道演出结束后观众久久不肯离去，而已届63岁的海顿在台上几度落泪。他在自己的笔记里写道：「观众热情使我深受感动。」这次演出也是他永别伦敦的最后纪念。

第104号交响曲被许多学者和指挥家认为是海顿全部交响曲中成就最高、结构最完美的一首，也被视为古典交响曲传统的绝对顶点之一。指挥家卡洛斯·克莱伯曾说：「在这里，海顿把能说的都说了，再也没有什么要补充的了。」作品的慢乐章以其深情的旋律线条和丰富的和声色彩，在海顿所有的慢乐章中首屈一指。
famousScores.detailEn:
Symphony No. 104 in D major 'London' was composed during Haydn's second London visit in 1795 and stands as the last of his 104 symphonies. A solemn slow introduction establishes an atmosphere of gravitas and anticipation before the sonata-form Allegro bursts forth with irresistible energy. The finale is built on a simple Croatian folk tune from Haydn's own rural memory, yet unfolds into a movement of grand symphonic sweep.

The premiere on May 4, 1795, at the King's Theatre was Haydn's farewell benefit concert in England. The performance was a triumphant success; Haydn reportedly wept on the podium, moved by the audience's enthusiasm. He noted in his diary: 'The audience was deeply moved.' At sixty-three, he was bidding farewell not only to London but to the symphony as a form he had spent his life perfecting.

Many scholars and conductors regard No. 104 as the single greatest of Haydn's symphonies — structurally the most perfect, emotionally the most complete. Carlos Kleiber famously remarked of it: 'Here Haydn has said everything there is to say; nothing needs to be added.' Its slow movement, with a deeply expressive melody over warmly colored harmonies, stands first among all of Haydn's many beautiful andantes.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Haydn Symphony 104 London Leonard Bernstein Vienna

famousScores.name: 《创世记》 Die Schöpfung
famousScores.desc: 清唱剧巨作，开篇的「混沌」到「光明」是音乐史上最伟大的时刻之一
famousScores.descEn: A monumental oratorio whose opening passage from Chaos to Light ranks among the greatest moments in music history
famousScores.detail:
《创世记》创作于1796-1798年，是海顿听了亨德尔的清唱剧后深受启发而创作的划时代作品。亨德尔的朋友林利为他提供了英语文本（综合了圣经创世纪与弥尔顿《失乐园》），他的友人和学生斯维滕男爵将其译为德语。海顿以65岁高龄开始创作，他晚年回忆说：「我每天跪下祈祷，上帝给了我这部作品。」

开篇「混沌的描绘」（The Representation of Chaos）是西方音乐史上最著名的序曲之一：不协和和弦的涌动、调性的漂移和音响的模糊共同描绘创世之前的混沌状态。随后合唱团突然唱出「And there was Light」（有了光），管弦乐队爆发出C大调明亮的全奏和弦——这一刻从混沌到光明的突变，被誉为音乐史上最震撼人心的音响瞬间之一。据传1799年维也纳首演时，这一段落引发了观众的惊叫声。

《创世记》首演于1798年4月29日，维也纳宫廷剧院的听众为之疯狂，74岁的海顿被听众抬着离开剧场。这部作品标志着海顿创作的最高峰，也是18世纪清唱剧传统的终极巅峰。海顿的两位弟子莫扎特和贝多芬都从这部作品中汲取了灵感：贝多芬的《第九交响曲》第四乐章大合唱的构思据说与《创世记》有直接的传承关系。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Creation was composed between 1796 and 1798, directly inspired by Handel's oratorios, which Haydn had heard in London. An English text (drawn from Genesis and Milton's Paradise Lost) was adapted for him, then translated into German by his patron Baron van Swieten. Haydn began the work at sixty-five and recalled in later life: 'I knelt down every day and prayed to God; He gave me this work.'

The opening 'Representation of Chaos' is one of the most celebrated orchestral prefaces in Western music: shifting dissonances, drifting tonality, and murky texture evoke the formless void before Creation. Then the chorus suddenly sings 'And there was Light,' and the orchestra blazes into a blazing C-major chord — the transition from darkness to light, widely regarded as one of the most electrifying single moments in all of music. At the Vienna premiere in 1799, this passage reportedly caused the audience to cry out in astonishment.

The public premiere in April 1798 reduced the Vienna audience to rapturous devotion; the seventy-four-year-old Haydn was reportedly carried out of the theater on the shoulders of admirers. The Creation represents the summit of Haydn's art and the ultimate pinnacle of the 18th-century oratorio tradition. Both Mozart and Beethoven drew inspiration from it: the choral finale of Beethoven's Ninth Symphony is widely seen as standing in direct creative lineage with Haydn's masterpiece.

famousScores.name: 弦乐四重奏 Op.76 「皇帝」
famousScores.desc: 其中的慢乐章旋律后来成为德国国歌
famousScores.descEn: Its slow-movement theme later became the German national anthem
famousScores.detail:
弦乐四重奏Op.76第三首（C大调「皇帝」）创作于1797年，是海顿同年献给奥地利皇帝弗朗茨二世生日的颂歌「神佑吾皇弗朗茨」（Gott erhalte Franz den Kaiser）的主题变奏。这首颂歌在当时立即成为奥地利帝国的非官方国歌，深受民众喜爱；而这首四重奏的慢乐章以首席小提琴呈示主题、随后其他三个声部以精心设计的变奏轮流接替，构成一套珠联璧合的主题与变奏。

这首颂歌旋律的命运轨迹令人唏嘘：1841年，诗人法勒斯莱本将其与「德意志高于一切」的歌词结合，使之成为德意志民族主义运动的象征，此后历经多次历史动荡——第一次世界大战、纳粹德国——这首旋律始终与德国身份认同深度绑定，直至今日仍是德意志联邦共和国的国歌（采用第三节歌词「统一、正义、自由」）。一位奥地利宫廷乐长的生日颂歌，竟成为德国精神的象征，这是海顿万万料想不到的。

在四重奏文献中，这套Op.76六首被认为是海顿最成熟的弦乐四重奏，连同Op.33与Op.64，共同构成海顿四重奏三大巅峰系列。「皇帝」慢乐章那种庄严温柔的情感——在对话与凝视之间徘徊——被认为是海顿人格最真实的音乐写照：虔诚、善良、从容，对美好事物怀有永不枯竭的感激。
famousScores.detailEn:
String Quartet Op. 76 No. 3 in C major 'Emperor' was composed in 1797 around a melody Haydn had just written as a birthday hymn for Emperor Franz II of Austria: 'Gott erhalte Franz den Kaiser' (God save Emperor Franz). The hymn became an instant unofficial national anthem beloved throughout the empire. In the quartet's slow movement, the first violin sings the melody in full while the remaining three voices provide increasingly elaborate accompanying variations, creating a perfect set of theme and variations.

The subsequent history of this melody is among the most extraordinary in all of music. In 1841, the poet Fallersleben set it to the words 'Deutschland über Alles,' linking it to German nationalist sentiment; it weathered two world wars and the Nazi era, emerging finally as the national anthem of the Federal Republic of Germany (using the third stanza, 'Unity, Justice, and Freedom'). That an Austrian court composer's birthday hymn for a Habsburg emperor should become the symbol of German nationhood was something Haydn could never have imagined.

Within the quartet literature, the six Op. 76 quartets are considered the summit of Haydn's achievement in the form, alongside Op. 33 and Op. 64. The 'Emperor' slow movement — suspended between dialogue and contemplation, dignified yet tender — is widely felt to be the most perfect musical self-portrait of Haydn's character: devout, generous, serene, and lastingly grateful for beauty.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Haydn Emperor Quartet Op.76 Amadeus Quartet

famousScores.name: 《小号协奏曲》降E大调
famousScores.desc: 古典协奏曲文献中的瑰宝，小号独奏的试金石
famousScores.descEn: A gem of the Classical concerto repertoire and the ultimate test piece for solo trumpet
famousScores.detail:
海顿的《降E大调小号协奏曲》创作于1796年，是他应维也纳宫廷小号手安东·魏丁格尔（Anton Weidinger）的请求而作。魏丁格尔刚发明了一种新式「键控小号」（Keyed Trumpet）——在小号管体上增加了类似风笛的按键，使其能够演奏出传统自然小号无法演奏的半音音阶。海顿为这一新乐器写下了前所未有的旋律：流畅的歌唱性曲调、大跳音程的优美线条，打破了小号此前局限于分解和弦的传统形象。

这部协奏曲有一段令人莞尔的历史：魏丁格尔在1800年维也纳首演时，台下的观众惊讶地发现小号居然能演奏出如此流畅的旋律，以为是在「作弊」——他们以为一定有某种木管乐器混在里面。然而这件为此曲专门发明的键控小号最终没能成为主流，不久之后活塞小号的出现取代了它的位置，这首协奏曲的独奏也因此改为用活塞小号演奏，直至今日。

海顿的这首协奏曲是小号协奏曲文献中迄今为止最重要的一首，代表着古典时代对铜管乐器写作方式的革命性突破。第二乐章的优美旋律堪比海顿最动人的慢乐章；终乐章的活力与优雅融为一体，令人叹服。每年各大国际音乐比赛的小号组别几乎都以这首协奏曲作为指定曲目，它是全世界每一位专业小号手必须驾驭的经典。
famousScores.detailEn:
Haydn's Trumpet Concerto in E-flat major was written in 1796 at the request of the Viennese court trumpeter Anton Weidinger, who had just invented the 'keyed trumpet' — an instrument fitted with keys similar to a flute, allowing it to play chromatic passages impossible on the natural (valveless) trumpet. Haydn exploited this new capability with delight, writing lyrical, singing melodies with wide leaps and stepwise chromatic lines that had never before been heard from a trumpet.

At the Vienna premiere in 1800, the audience was reportedly baffled by the trumpet's newfound ability to sing so smoothly, suspecting that an oboe must be hidden somewhere in the orchestra. Ironically, the keyed trumpet never became standard equipment: the invention of the valved trumpet shortly afterward rendered Weidinger's instrument obsolete, and the concerto has since been performed on the valved trumpet for which it was not actually written.

Haydn's concerto remains the single most important work in the trumpet repertoire, representing a revolutionary expansion of brass writing within the Classical style. Its slow movement equals the most beautiful of Haydn's many gorgeous andantes; the finale blends brilliance with a dancing grace that is quintessentially Haydnesque. It is required repertoire for virtually every international trumpet competition, and a concerto that every professional trumpeter in the world must master.

famousScores.name: 《四季》 Die Jahreszeiten
famousScores.desc: 海顿最后一部大型声乐作品，对大自然四季的壮丽描绘
famousScores.descEn: Haydn's final large-scale vocal work — a magnificent celebration of nature through the four seasons
famousScores.detail:
《四季》清唱剧创作于1799-1801年，是海顿《创世记》之后紧接着的另一部清唱剧巨作，同样由斯维滕男爵提供剧本（译自苏格兰诗人詹姆斯·汤姆森的叙事长诗）。全曲分为春、夏、秋、冬四个部分，以农民一家的田园生活为线索，将四季更替的自然景象用音乐一一描绘：春雨、夏日雷暴、秋天丰收的喜悦以及冬夜严寒中炉旁讲故事的温馨场景。

创作这部作品几乎耗尽了海顿的生命。年届68岁的他在写完《四季》后说：「《四季》把我毁了。」漫长的创作过程以及斯维滕对他无休止的催促使他精疲力竭，此后他再未完成任何大型作品。1801年的维也纳首演大获成功，但海顿已在第一次排练结束后称自己「感到虚弱极了」。这部作品成为他天鹅之歌般的告别之作。

《四季》中有无数精妙的音画段落：夏季乐章的雷暴用弦乐快速跑动、定音鼓滚奏和铜管齐鸣描绘大雷雨；秋天的狩猎场景以猎号呼应、追逐音型和最终胜利的歌声表达热情的野趣；冬天的「纺纱歌」则是古典音乐中最动人的家庭温情写照之一。贝多芬的《田园》交响曲显然从海顿的音画传统中汲取了直接灵感，这种自然描绘传统一直延伸至马勒的大地之歌。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Seasons was composed between 1799 and 1801 — Haydn's second great oratorio, following The Creation, with a libretto by Baron van Swieten adapted from the Scottish poet James Thomson's narrative poem. The work is divided into Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter, following the lives of farm folk through the year: spring showers, a violent summer thunderstorm, the joys of harvest, and the intimate warmth of a winter fireside story-telling scene.

The work nearly killed its creator. After completing it, the sixty-eight-year-old Haydn said: 'The Seasons has finished me.' The protracted effort and van Swieten's relentless pressure had drained his remaining strength; he never completed another major work. The Vienna premiere of 1801 was a triumphant success, but Haydn told friends after the first rehearsal that he felt 'utterly exhausted.' It became his final testament.

The Seasons contains some of Haydn's most vivid pictorial music: the Summer thunderstorm (strings racing, drums rolling, brass thundering); the Autumn hunting scene with horn calls and pursuing figures; and the Winter 'Spinning Song,' one of the warmest domestic scenes in all of Classical music. Beethoven's Pastoral Symphony draws directly on the pictorial tradition Haydn established here, and that tradition extends forward through Mahler's Das Lied von der Erde to the nature-music of the 20th century.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Haydn Seasons Die Jahreszeiten Antal Dorati

famousScores.name: 《第45号交响曲「告别」》
famousScores.desc: 终曲中乐手们依次离场，是音乐史上最机智的抗议方式
famousScores.descEn: Its finale sees musicians depart one by one — the most witty act of protest in music history
famousScores.detail:
《第45号交响曲》（升f小调「告别」）创作于1772年，是海顿在埃斯特哈齐宫廷最具戏剧性的作品。背景是：亲王尼古劳斯·埃斯特哈齐在匈牙利夏宫长住不归，令宫廷乐师们苦不堪言——他们的妻子与家人在埃斯特哈泽只能呆上有限的时间，滞留日久的乐师们渴望回家，却不敢直接向亲王提出。海顿用音乐代替了他们的请愿。

终曲乐章到达一半时，音乐从急板突然变成极慢的柔和段落，接着乐手们一个接一个地吹灭谱架上的蜡烛、收好乐器、退出演奏台，最后只剩两把小提琴在夜色中低声呜咽。据记载，亲王当即领会了这个无声的信号，第二天便宣布乐团可以回家休假了。海顿不费一字，用音乐实现了一次精妙的外交斡旋。

「告别」交响曲在音乐史上具有深远的象征意义，它展示了海顿在埃斯特哈齐宫廷时代最具个性的一面：既是忠实的宫廷乐长，又从未放弃保护手下音乐家权益的责任感。这首交响曲以升f小调写成，那个时代这一调性极为罕见，用以表达渴望与忧郁的情绪。整部作品在「斯图姆与德朗」（疾风怒涛）风格的影响下创作，是海顿情感最直接、最个人化的交响曲之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Symphony No. 45 in F-sharp minor 'Farewell' was composed in 1772 under circumstances as unusual as any in music history. Prince Nikolaus Esterházy had extended his stay at his Hungarian summer palace well beyond the expected season, leaving the court musicians separated from their wives and families and desperate to return home — yet too intimidated to request leave directly. Haydn made their petition for them through the music.

In the finale, after a passage of driving Allegro, the music shifts suddenly into a hushed Adagio, and the players begin to extinguish their candles one by one, pack up their instruments, and leave the stage — until only two solo violins remain, murmuring softly in the darkness. The prince reportedly understood the hint immediately; the very next day he announced that the orchestra could return home. Haydn had conducted an act of musical diplomacy without uttering a single word.

The 'Farewell' Symphony has lasting symbolic significance in music history as an example of Haydn's responsibility toward the musicians in his charge. Written in the rare key of F-sharp minor — evoking yearning and melancholy — it is one of the most deeply personal of Haydn's symphonies, composed under the influence of the Sturm und Drang literary movement and revealing an emotional directness quite different from his usual wit and urbanity.

famousScores.name: 《降E大调钢琴奏鸣曲》 Hob.XVI/52
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Sonata in E-flat major, Hob. XVI/52
famousScores.desc: 海顿最伟大的钢琴奏鸣曲，气势与细腻兼备
famousScores.descEn: Haydn's greatest piano sonata, combining grandeur with extraordinary delicacy
famousScores.detail:
《降E大调钢琴奏鸣曲》Hob.XVI/52是海顿于1794-1795年第二次伦敦之行期间创作的，献给英国钢琴家特蕾莎·简森（后来的巴泰曼夫人）。这是海顿62首钢琴奏鸣曲中公认的巅峰之作。首乐章主部主题在两只手之间大幅跳跃，充满了舒展的贵族气质；慢乐章以E大调写成，那种明亮纯净的色彩在整个降E大调框架中产生一种令人心醉的调性对比。

这部奏鸣曲有一个耐人寻味的特点：它与莫扎特、克莱门蒂为代表的维也纳-古典钢琴风格大相径庭，倒更预示了贝多芬晚期奏鸣曲的宏大格局与情感复杂度。海顿写作此曲时，已经目睹了第一代维也纳古典乐派的成型，同时也正是在伦敦汲取了英国钢琴演奏风格（以克莱门蒂为代表）的新鲜营养，两者的综合使这首奏鸣曲具有了超越时代的分量。

终乐章（急板）以强烈对比的音型制造出一种几近即兴的戏剧感——钢琴在不同音区之间跳跃，如同一场内心独白。指挥家兼钢琴家阿尔弗雷德·布伦德尔多次将这首奏鸣曲列入他的重要曲目，并在文章中详细分析其结构独创性，认为它在钢琴奏鸣曲文献中是海顿留给贝多芬的最重要遗产之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Piano Sonata in E-flat major Hob. XVI/52 was composed during Haydn's second London visit (1794–1795), dedicated to the English pianist Therese Jansen (later Mrs. Bartolozzi). It is universally acknowledged as the summit of Haydn's sixty-two piano sonatas. The first movement's main theme leaps boldly across both hands with aristocratic expansiveness; the slow movement, in the unexpectedly bright key of E major, creates a breathtaking tonal contrast within the overall E-flat major framework.

What makes this sonata particularly striking is how unlike it is from the prevailing Viennese-Classical piano style of Mozart and Clementi — how strikingly it anticipates the grand scale and emotional complexity of Beethoven's late sonatas. Haydn wrote it having witnessed the full formation of the Viennese Classical style, while simultaneously absorbing the fresh influence of the English pianistic tradition (represented above all by Clementi) during his London years. The synthesis gave the work a weight beyond its time.

The finale (Presto) creates an almost improvisatory dramatic excitement through extreme contrasts between registers — the piano leaping between treble and bass as if engaged in an interior monologue. Pianist and conductor Alfred Brendel has repeatedly performed this sonata and analyzed its structural originality in detail, calling it one of the most important legacies Haydn left Beethoven in the piano sonata tradition.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Haydn Piano Sonata Hob XVI 52 Alfred Brendel

famousScores.name: 弦乐四重奏 Op.33 No.2 「玩笑」
famousScores.desc: 终曲以最机智的假结尾让听众哑然失笑，是音乐幽默的极致
famousScores.descEn: Its finale tricks the audience with the most ingenious false ending in music — the pinnacle of musical humor
famousScores.detail:
弦乐四重奏Op.33第二首（降E大调「玩笑」）是海顿1781年「俄国四重奏」系列中的一首，这套作品本身也是海顿自我评价甚高的创新之作——他在写给出版商的信中自豪地称之为「以全新的、特别的方式创作的」，意指他在其中大量使用了诙谐曲（Scherzo）代替了传统的小步舞曲。然而这套四重奏中最著名的「玩笑」来自终乐章。

终曲（回旋曲）的主题简单明快，多次反复后，音乐突然停顿，随后每隔几小节出现一次——每次都让听众以为终于结束了，可以鼓掌了，然而音乐又鬼使神差地重新响起。海顿制造了四次「假结尾」，每次停顿后的沉默都越来越长，越来越考验听众的耐性。据记载，1781年首演时，维也纳听众在最后一次停顿时已经忍不住开始鼓掌，音乐再次响起时爆发出哄堂大笑。

Op.33这套六首四重奏在海顿四重奏创作史上具有转折性的意义：他在信中指出已经「完全抛弃了」此前的写法，学界认为这是奏鸣曲式在四重奏中真正成熟的标志。莫扎特在听到这套作品后，专门创作了他的「海顿四重奏」K.387-465（六首，1782-1785年）作为回应，并将它们献给海顿，这是音乐史上最著名的创作致敬之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
String Quartet Op. 33 No. 2 in E-flat major 'The Joke' is one of Haydn's 1781 'Russian Quartets,' a set he described in a letter to his publisher with proud self-regard as 'composed in a new, very special manner' — referring to his replacement of the traditional minuet with a Scherzo. But the most celebrated joke in the set comes in the finale.

The finale (Rondo) has a simple, cheerful main theme that returns several times before the music begins its series of deceptions: it stops, the theme reappears after a few bars, then stops again — each time tricking the audience into thinking it is over, prompting them to begin applauding, only for the music to return one more time. Haydn engineers four false endings, with each silence growing longer and more pointed. At the 1781 Vienna premiere, the audience reportedly began applauding at the last pause, only to dissolve into laughter when the theme came back once more.

The Op. 33 set marks a turning point in Haydn's quartet writing: he declared he had 'abandoned' his previous approach entirely, and scholars regard these works as the moment when sonata form truly matured within the quartet genre. Mozart, upon hearing them, composed his own six 'Haydn Quartets' (K. 387–465, 1782–1785) as a direct creative response, dedicating them to Haydn — one of the most celebrated acts of musical homage in history.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Haydn String Quartet Op.33 Kodály Quartet

famousScores.name: 《七言》 Die sieben letzten Worte unseres Erlösers am Kreuze
famousScores.nameEn: The Seven Last Words of Our Saviour on the Cross
famousScores.desc: 七首沉思乐章，深邃宁静，是海顿最具精神深度的弦乐四重奏版本
famousScores.descEn: Seven meditative movements of profound spiritual depth — Haydn's most spiritually searching work
famousScores.detail:
《基督在十字架上的七言》最初于1787年以管弦乐组曲形式创作，是海顿应西班牙加的斯大教堂之邀为耶稣受难周礼拜而作。七首「奏鸣曲」（Sonata）分别对应耶稣在十字架上的七句遗言，每首均以慢板写成，全曲没有任何快速段落，纯粹的冥想性音乐。海顿后来将其改编为弦乐四重奏版（1787）、钢琴版和清唱剧版（1796），其中弦乐四重奏版被认为是室内乐版本中最深刻的一个。

这部作品有着极不寻常的音乐会背景：加的斯大教堂是西班牙式的朴素石室，在受难礼拜期间，主教宣读每一句「七言」后便退场，让音乐在黑暗、点着蜡烛的教堂中回响，而会众在整整两小时内保持绝对静默。据海顿自述，他在接受委约时颇感棘手——如何在纯器乐中持续保持七段慢乐章的精神紧张性，而不流于单调？

海顿解决这一挑战的方式令人叹服：每首「奏鸣曲」都有截然不同的性格和调性色彩，情绪从深沉的悲痛、宁静的接受到最后一声雷霆「地动山摇」（地震乐章Terremoto，急板强奏）不等。最后这段地震音乐以其突然的爆发打破了整部作品的冥想宁静，如同信仰突然被现实震撼。布伦德尔将这部作品称为「海顿最被低估的杰作」，勃拉姆斯则据说将弦乐四重奏版视为室内乐文献中最令人难忘的精神体验之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Seven Last Words of Our Saviour on the Cross was originally composed in 1787 as an orchestral suite, commissioned by the cathedral of Cádiz, Spain, for its Good Friday service. The seven 'Sonatas' correspond to Christ's seven utterances from the cross, each marked Adagio — the entire work unfolds without a single fast movement, in sustained meditation. Haydn later arranged it for string quartet (1787), for keyboard, and as an oratorio (1796); the string quartet version is widely regarded as the most profoundly moving of all.

The original concert setting was extraordinary: the cathedral of Cádiz was a bare Spanish stone chamber; after the bishop read each 'Last Word,' he withdrew, leaving the music to resonate in candlelit darkness before a congregation that maintained complete silence for a full two hours. Haydn later recalled that when he received the commission, the challenge perplexed him: how could seven consecutive slow movements sustain spiritual intensity without monotony?

His solution is masterly: each Sonata has a sharply distinct character and harmonic color, moving from deep grief through serene acceptance to the final cataclysm — the earthquake movement (Terremoto), a sudden fortissimo eruption that shatters the work's meditative hush like a thunderbolt of reality breaking through prayer. Brendel has called this work 'Haydn's most underrated masterpiece'; Brahms reportedly regarded the string quartet version as among the most unforgettable spiritual experiences in the chamber music literature.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Haydn Seven Last Words String Quartet Amadeus

influence: 海顿对莫扎特和贝多芬的影响是直接而深刻的——莫扎特称他为「最亲爱的朋友和老师」，贝多芬曾短暂师从海顿。他确立的奏鸣曲式和主题发展原则成为此后两百年交响乐创作的基石。弦乐四重奏在他手中成为最精密的室内乐形式，勃拉姆斯、巴托克、肖斯塔科维奇都在这一传统中继续探索。
influenceEn: Haydn's influence on Mozart and Beethoven was direct and profound — Mozart called him 'my dearest friend and teacher,' and Beethoven briefly studied under him. The sonata-form principles and thematic development techniques he established became the bedrock of symphonic composition for the next two centuries. Under his hands the string quartet became the most refined form of chamber music, a tradition carried forward by Brahms, Bartok, and Shostakovich.
